payload.name: Trust
payload.description: A strict Rust dialect for the bugs LLMs actually ship — strict lints + named-args and pipe (|>) syntax extensions, lowered to plain Rust. Catches the agent bug classes Clippy can't (same-typed positional-arg swaps) plus the ones it can (unwrap, as-casts, globs).
payload.overview:
# Trust

A **strict Rust dialect for the bugs LLMs actually ship** (formerly Rustricted).

Agents write Rust that compiles, type-checks, and reviews clean — then ship a small, predictable set of bugs: positional-argument swaps, `.unwrap()` in production paths, `as` casts that silently truncate, glob imports. Add `#![strict]` and those become compile errors with a fix in the message.

## What it is

A thin frontend over **stable Rust**. Two grammar extensions — named arguments and the pipe `|>` — lower to plain positional Rust before `rustc` sees the file; the lints are ordinary static checks. Stop using Trust tomorrow and the lowered code still builds.

## The moat over Rust + Clippy

The one thing Clippy structurally cannot do: catch same-typed positional-argument swaps, because the fix needs syntax Rust lacks (names at the call site). Most strict lints have Clippy analogues — the real differentiators are the frontend, a cross-crate signature index, and an agent-tuned diagnostic contract.

## Status

0.1 — agent-authored, eval-backed prototype. 18 rules, working CLI, RUSTC_WRAPPER/RUSTDOC shims, LSP. MIT OR Apache-2.0. Post-0.1 technical roadmap is RT-62..RT-74 (Tiers 0–3).
